name: Creole Smoked Sausage and Creole Hot Sausage
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Lean pork (or 2 lb lean pork and 2 lb lean beef)
recipeInstructions:
1. New Orleans' most popular sausage, a type of country sausage made with pork or pork and beef. It's not really smoked, but has a fine smoky flavor that makes it an ideal seasoning meat for our favorite bean dishes, gumbos, and jambalayas. Pan-grilled as a breakfast or dinner sausage. Allow about 20 to 25 minutes for grilling.
2. When used as a seasoning meat in other dishes, no precooking is required.
3. About 6 pounds of 6-8 inch sausages (To make HOT sausage, omit the liquid hickory smoke and add 1 tsp cayenne and 1 tsp black pepper.)
4. Hot sausage pairs well with bean dishes or smothered vegetables or as a breakfast sausage with grits and eggs.
5. Not recommended as a seasoning meat in traditional bean dishes as it may overpower other flavors.
6. Prepare the sausage casings by cleaning them thoroughly, then tie into 12-inch lengths. Stuff the casings using a sausage stuffer or needle injector, ensuring the stuffing is slightly coarse in texture.
